title: Ruehssia dictyophylla
intro: Ruehssia dictyophylla, also known as Himalayan Rue, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ruehssia dictyophylla include alkaloids, glycosides, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions and skin problems.
